# Let's attempt to read the file /mnt/data/text_to_analyse.txt. If not present, use the provided text in the prompt.
import os, re, json, textwrap, sys, unicodedata, io

file_path = "/mnt/data/text_to_analyse.txt"
if os.path.exists(file_path):
    with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        text = f.read()
else:
    text = """\"صلى الله عليه وسلم\" هي تعبير يُستخدم لتعظيم الله تعالى وتنبيه الناس من تقليد الصلاة على النبي محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم. يُستخدم هذا التعبير بعد ذكر اسم النبي محمد في أي حوار أو كتاب أو رسالة.
معنى \"صلى الله عليه وسلم\" هو:
- \"صلى\" يُستخدم لتعظيم الله تعالى، مما يعني \"تبارك\" أو \"تعالى\" في بعض المعاني.
- \"الله\" هو الاسم المطلق الذي يُستخدم لتعريف الله تعالى.
- \"عليه\" يعني \"عليه السلام\"، وهو تعبير يُستخدم لتعظيم النبي محمد وبيت عائله.
- \"وسلم\" يعني \"وسلام\"، وهو تعبير يُستخدم لتعظيم الله تعالى وتنبيه الناس من تقليد السلام على النبي محمد.
تستند هذه التعبير إلى آيات من القرآن الكريم وأحاديث شريفة، مثل:
- قوله تعالى: \"صَلِّ عَلَى النَبِيِّ وَعَلَى أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ\" (سورة الأحزاب: 56).
- قوله تعالى: \"وَإِذَا مَرِضْتُ فَلا رُكُبَ عَلَيَّ وَلا حَمِيَةَ وَأَصْبَحَ بَياضٌ\" (سورة التغابن: 2-3). (في هذه الآية، يُستخدم تعبير \"صَلِّ عَلَيَّ\" لتعظيم الله تعالى).
- قوله تعالى: \"وَمَن يَقُلْ لِوَليٍّ هَذَا عَقِدَ لِيَ عَلَيْهِمْ عُقْدَةً مِنَ اللَهِ\" (سورة الطلاق: 3). (في هذه الآية، يُستخدم تعبير \"عَلَيْهِمْ\" لتعظيم الله تعالى).
- قوله تعالى: \"وَلَوْلا دَعْوَةُ هَارُونَ لَبَلَغَتِ الْأَرْضُ مُصْلًا وَإِنَافًا\" (سورة القصص: 24). (في هذه الآية، يُستخدم تعبير \"لَبَلَغَتِ الْأَرْضُ مُصْلًا وَإِنَافًا\" لتعظيم الله تعالى).
- قوله تعالى: \"وَلا تَقُل لَّم أَجِدْ شَيْئًا\" (سورة البقرة: 187). (في هذه الآية، يُستخدم تعبير \"لا تَقُل\" لتنبيه الناس من التقليد).
- روى البخاري عن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم قال: \"لا تَزُدوا في الصلاة، وَلا تُقَصِّروا، وَلا تَقُوموا عَلَيها حَتّى تُكْمِلوا\" (رواه البخاري).
- روى مسلم عن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم قال: \"إِنَّ اللَهَ يُحِبُّ الصَفَواتِ، وَيُحِبُّ الصَفَواتِ في الصَلاةِ\" (رواه مسلم).
هذه بعض الآيات والأحاديث التي تستند عليها تعبير \"صلى الله عليه وسلم\"."""

# Let's extract all quoted spans that are in the citations list (after the 'تستند هذه التعبير .. مثل:' marker)
start_marker = "تستند هذه التعبير إلى آيات من القرآن الكريم وأحاديث شريفة، مثل:"
start_idx = text.find(start_marker)
len(text), start_idx, text[start_idx:start_idx+50]

import re

section = text[start_idx:]
# find all quoted strings inside that section lines that contain قوله تعالى or روى
pattern = r'\"([^\"]+)\"'
matches = list(re.finditer(pattern, section))
len(matches), [m.group(1) for m in matches[:10]]

# Precompute starting index of each line in the full text
line_starts = []
pos = 0
for line in lines:
    line_starts.append(pos)
    pos += len(line) + 1  # +1 for the newline splitlines kept (assuming \n)
# For each cit_line, extract quoted phrase and compute spans
citations = []
for i, line in cit_lines:
    abs_start_line = line_starts[i]
    # find first quoted block after the colon in this line
    m = re.search(r'\"([^\"]+)\"', line)
    if m:
        quote_text = m.group(1)
        # start and end indices relative to whole text
        rel_start = m.start(1)
        rel_end = m.end(1)
        span_start = abs_start_line + rel_start
        span_end = abs_start_line + rel_end
        citations.append({
            "line_index": i,
            "line": line,
            "quote": quote_text,
            "span_start": span_start,
            "span_end": span_end
        })
len(citations), citations

output.code:
# Build the citations list structure according to our decisions.
# We have computed citations with spans: list "citations" variable earlier.
citations_data = citations  # contains entries with quote, span_start, span_end

# Build the output entries
output_entries = []
for idx, c in enumerate(citations_data):
    quote = c["quote"]
    start = c["span_start"]
    end = c["span_end"]
    # Determine the type based on line: if line starts with "- قوله تعالى" => Aya, else Hadeeth
    line = c["line"]
    if "قوله تعالى" in line:
        citation_type = "Aya"
        # Determine sura from the parenthetical part
        # Extract content inside parentheses after the quoted text
        # We'll extract the first parenthetical portion after the quote that starts with "(سورة ..."
        sura_match = None
        # Create a string starting from the end of the quote to end of line
        quote_end_idx = line.find('"' + quote + '"') + len('"' + quote + '"')
        trailing = line[quote_end_idx:]
        m = re.search(r'سورة\s+([^\):]+)\s*:\s*([0-9]+)(?:-\s*([0-9]+))?', trailing)
        sura_name = None
        sura_number = None
        aya_num = None
        if m:
            sura_name = m.group(1).strip()
            # Map sura name to number where possible; here we can just hardcode for those we know.
            # We'll fill numbers based on known standard: الأحزاب=33, التغابن=64, الطلاق=65, القصص=28, البقرة=2
            mapping = {"الأحزاب":33, "التغابن":64, "الطلاق":65, "القصص":28, "البقرة":2}
            sura_number = mapping.get(sura_name, None)
            if m.group(3):
                # range, take the first number for aya_number
                aya_num = int(m.group(2))
            else:
                aya_num = int(m.group(2))
        # Determine validity and corrected text based on which line index
        is_valid = False
        completely_invalid = False
        corrected_text = None
        aya_reference = {"sura_name": sura_name, "sura_number": sura_number, "aya_number": aya_num}
        # Cases per line index
        if c["line_index"] == 7:
            # Ahzab 33:56; correct to actual verse
            is_valid = False
            completely_invalid = False
            corrected_text = "إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَمَلَائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ۚ يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا"
            aya_reference = {"sura_name":"الأحزاب", "sura_number":33, "aya_number":56}
        elif c["line_index"] == 8:
            # Taghabun 2-3 bogus phrase 
            is_valid = False
            completely_invalid = True
            corrected_text = "خطأ"
            # Keep reference as Taghabun claimed, but it's a range; we used 2
            aya_reference = {"sura_name":"التغابن", "sura_number":64, "aya_number":2}
        elif c["line_index"] == 9:
            # Talaq 3 bogus phrase; correct to 65:3
            is_valid = False
            completely_invalid = False
            corrected_text = "وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ ۚ وَمَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَالِغُ أَمْرِهِ ۚ قَدْ جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدْرًا"
            aya_reference = {"sura_name":"الطلاق", "sura_number":65, "aya_number":3}
        elif c["line_index"] == 10:
            # Qasas 24 bogus; mark invalid completely
            is_valid = False
            completely_invalid = True
            corrected_text = "خطأ"
            aya_reference = {"sura_name":"القصص", "sura_number":28, "aya_number":24}
        elif c["line_index"] == 11:
            # Baqara 187 bogus; invalid completely
            is_valid = False
            completely_invalid = True
            corrected_text = "خطأ"
            aya_reference = {"sura_name":"البقرة", "sura_number":2, "aya_number":187}
        output_entries.append({
            "span_start": start,
            "span_end": end,
            "span_text": quote,
            "citation_type": citation_type,
            "is_valid": is_valid,
            "aya_reference": aya_reference,
            "hadeeth_reference": {},
            "corrected_text": corrected_text,
            "completely_invalid": completely_invalid
        })
    else:
        # Hadeeth
        citation_type = "Hadeeth"
        # For both hadith, we mark invalid completely
        is_valid = False
        completely_invalid = True
        corrected_text = "خطأ"
        output_entries.append({
            "span_start": start,
            "span_end": end,
            "span_text": quote,
            "citation_type": citation_type,
            "is_valid": is_valid,
            "aya_reference": {},
            "hadeeth_reference": {},
            "corrected_text": corrected_text,
            "completely_invalid": completely_invalid
        })

len(output_entries), output_entries[:2]

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
